mnemonics.title: Algorithm Order
mnemonics.content: FSPR: 'First Students Prefer Reading' → FCFS, SJF, Priority, Round Robin (sequence of algorithm complexity)

mnemonics.title: Scheduling Criteria
mnemonics.content: Cats Twirl Toys While Relaxing: CPU Utilization, Throughput, Turnaround, Waiting Time, Response Time (CUTWR criteria order)

mnemonics.title: SJF Trade-off
mnemonics.content: Short Jobs Fast, But Starve: SJF minimizes waiting time but may cause starvation for long processes

mnemonics.title: Round Robin Operation
mnemonics.content: Round Robin Rotates Rapidly: Visualize CPU as a rotating turntable serving each process in fixed time slots

mnemonics.title: Priority Scheduling
mnemonics.content: High Priority Hurries Processes: Higher priority numbers (or values) get immediate attention

acronyms.expansion: CPU Utilization, Throughput, Turnaround, Waiting, Response
acronyms.usage: Remember the 5 key scheduling evaluation criteria
Critical parameter determining context switch frequency
Algorithm name and its primary selection criteria

visualTips.visualization: Imagine people boarding a bus - first in line gets on first, others wait patiently
Picture a laundry queue sorted by garment size - smallest items processed first
Visualize a pizza sliced equally, each slice representing a time quantum
Think of airport security lanes - VIPs get expedited processing
A process fading away in the background while newer processes keep jumping ahead

analogies.analogy: Like a grocery store checkout - customers are served in exact arrival order regardless of cart size
Express package sorting - prioritize small parcels to clear maximum items quickly
Speed dating event - each participant gets equal time before rotating to next
Hospital triage system - critical patients get immediate attention over routine cases
